disclaimer: Permit requirements change frequently. This information is provided as a general guide only. Always verify current requirements with your local building/zoning department before starting any fence project. Ghareeb Fencing Solutions handles all permit research and applications as part of our installation service.
defaultNote: Contact your local building department to verify current requirements before starting your fence project.

municipalities.name: Toledo
municipalities.applicationProcess: Apply online at toledo.oh.gov or in person at One Government Center

heightRestrictions.cornerLot: Sight triangle — reduced height near intersections. Corner lots have two front yards.

municipalities.setbackRequirements: Fences on or behind property lines. No part of fence in public right-of-way. Survey recommended.
municipalities.materialRestrictions: No barbed wire or electrified fences in residential zones. No used/discarded materials (pallets, doors, tires, corrugated metal). Finished side must face adjacent property. Posts must be treated lumber.
municipalities.additionalNotes: ALL fences require Certificate of Zoning Compliance regardless of height. Repairs under 100 sq ft matching existing material are exempt. Fences under 6 ft do NOT need a building permit, only the zoning certificate.

contactInfo.address: One Government Center, Suite 1600, Toledo, OH 43604

municipalities.name: Maumee
municipalities.applicationProcess: Apply through Maumee Building Department. Zoning questions: Gina Schell (419) 897-7171
municipalities.setbackRequirements: Fences may be placed on property line outside public right-of-way. New fence within 5 ft of existing fence requires removal of old fence first.
municipalities.materialRestrictions: Contact building department for approved materials. Fence posts cannot exceed fence height by more than 4 inches.
municipalities.additionalNotes: Historic Uptown district (Ford St to Key St, Anthony Wayne Trail to Maumee River) requires Design Review Board approval before permits.

municipalities.name: Sylvania
municipalities.applicationProcess: Zoning permit from City of Sylvania required before obtaining building permit from Lucas County

heightRestrictions.sideYard: 4 ft max (adjacent to main building); 7 ft max elsewhere
heightRestrictions.cornerLot: No obstruction within 80 ft of intersection centerlines

municipalities.setbackRequirements: Fences on or behind property lines
municipalities.materialRestrictions: Contact zoning administrator for approved materials
municipalities.additionalNotes: Many neighborhoods have HOAs with additional requirements. Pool fences: public pools require 6 ft fencing; private pools require 4 ft fencing with self-closing/locking gates.

municipalities.name: Sylvania Township
municipalities.applicationProcess: Apply through Sylvania Township Zoning Department
municipalities.additionalNotes: Separate jurisdiction from City of Sylvania. Properties with well/septic need Lucas County Health Dept approval first.

municipalities.name: Oregon
municipalities.applicationProcess: Apply through Oregon Building Department
municipalities.setbackRequirements: No survey required for fence installation per city FAQ. Finished side must face adjacent property.
municipalities.additionalNotes: Waterfront properties may have additional requirements

municipalities.name: Ottawa Hills
municipalities.applicationProcess: Apply through Ottawa Hills Village Hall
municipalities.materialRestrictions: Upscale community — may have aesthetic requirements
municipalities.additionalNotes: Small, upscale community with potentially stricter aesthetic guidelines

municipalities.name: Waterville
municipalities.applicationProcess: Apply through Waterville Building Department
municipalities.materialRestrictions: Historic district may have specific requirements
municipalities.additionalNotes: Historic downtown area may have additional architectural review

municipalities.name: Holland
municipalities.applicationProcess: Apply at Village offices, 1245 Clarion Avenue. Building permits through Lucas County Building Regulations.
municipalities.setbackRequirements: Submit drawing showing residence, accessory buildings, fences with distances from street and property lines
municipalities.additionalNotes: Large Holland mailing address area — only some addresses are actually under Village jurisdiction. Verify your address is in the Village before applying. Pool fences: min 42 inches, self-locking gate required.

municipalities.name: Springfield Twp
municipalities.applicationProcess: No fence permit typically required in unincorporated areas. Always call 811 before digging.
municipalities.setbackRequirements: No zoning setback requirements — property line rules apply. Survey recommended.
municipalities.materialRestrictions: No specific restrictions in unincorporated areas
municipalities.additionalNotes: Unincorporated township — generally no fence permit required. HOA rules may still apply. Note: Holland Village (within Springfield Twp) DOES require a zoning permit.

municipalities.name: Washington Twp
municipalities.applicationProcess: No fence permit typically required. Always call 811 before digging.
municipalities.setbackRequirements: No zoning setback requirements. Survey recommended.
municipalities.additionalNotes: Unincorporated township — no fence permit generally required. HOA rules may still apply.

municipalities.applicationProcess: No fence permit typically required. Always call 811 before digging.
municipalities.setbackRequirements: No zoning setback requirements. Survey recommended.
municipalities.additionalNotes: Unincorporated township — no fence permit generally required.

municipalities.setbackRequirements: No zoning setback requirements. Survey recommended.
municipalities.additionalNotes: Unincorporated township — no fence permit generally required. HOA rules may still apply.

municipalities.name: Perrysburg
municipalities.applicationProcess: City contracts with Wood County Building Inspection for permits
municipalities.setbackRequirements: Fences on property line or within property boundaries
municipalities.additionalNotes: Historic district may have additional requirements. HOA rules may also apply.

contactInfo.department: City of Perrysburg / Wood County Building Inspection

municipalities.name: Perrysburg Twp
municipalities.applicationProcess: Township is PARTIALLY ZONED. Call with your property address to determine if permits are required.
municipalities.setbackRequirements: Varies by zoning status of property
municipalities.additionalNotes: IMPORTANT: Perrysburg Township is PARTIALLY ZONED. Some areas require permits, others do not. Call the township office with your property address.

municipalities.name: Monroe
municipalities.applicationProcess: Apply through Monroe Building Department at City Hall. Submit site plan with property dimensions, building locations, fence location/amount/style/height.

heightRestrictions.cornerLot: No sight obstruction at intersections or driveways per city engineer

municipalities.setbackRequirements: Fences/hedges may be in city right-of-way up to sidewalk, but owner assumes risk. Height measured from ground on which fence stands.
municipalities.materialRestrictions: No electric fences (except for agricultural nonconforming use). No barbed wire except with city engineer approval for security. Lots 1+ acre may have 6 ft max anywhere if no sight obstruction.
municipalities.additionalNotes: Michigan building codes apply — different from Ohio. Contractor must be State of Michigan licensed. Fence on retaining wall measured from uphill side.

municipalities.additionalNotes: Michigan regulations apply. Includes Temperance and Lambertville communities.

generalTips.survey: A professional survey is recommended for any uncertainty.
generalTips.utilities: Ohio law requires calling 811 before digging. We handle this as part of our installation process.
generalTips.hoa: If you live in a neighborhood with a Homeowners Association, check with them for any additional restrictions or approval requirements.
generalTips.neighbors: While not legally required in most cases, discussing your fence plans with neighbors can prevent disputes.
generalTips.timing: Permit processing times vary. Plan ahead, especially during busy spring and summer months.

ctaDescription: Ghareeb Fencing Solutions handles all permit applications as part of our full-service installation. We know the requirements for every municipality in our service area.
